differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE) with Antiphospholipid Syndrome (APS) and Catastrophic APS
Disseminated malignancy with non-bacterial thrombotic endocarditis
Rocky Mountain Spotted Fever or other tick-borne rickettsial disease
next_steps: Check serum complement levels (C3, C4) and anti-dsDNA antibodies
Order antiphospholipid antibody panel (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin antibodies, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Perform echocardiography (transesophageal preferred) to evaluate for valvular vegetations
Measure ADAMTS13 activity level to rule out TTP
Send serum and CSF for specialized infectious disease testing (e.g., Rickettsia serology, Leptospira PCR)
Avoid anticoagulation until TTP and intracranial hemorrhage are definitively managed or excluded
Consider skin biopsy of the petechial lesions for pathology and immunofluorescence
